Empires & Puzzles Jolly Review: The 3-Star Rush Attack God

Don't let the 'Very Slow' speed fool you. In the right format, Jolly is an absolute monster. With massive overhealing, damage reduction, and mana speed buffs, he rivals the legendary Mother North in impact—but for 3-star tournaments. We break down why this little lumberjack deserves a spot in your roster.
Jolly: The Gnome Lumberjack Who Breaks Rush Attack
Jolly, The Gnome Lumberjack: Rush Attack God
Meet Jolly, the 3-star Holy hero who proves that good things come to those who wait—or to those who play Rush Attack. Hailing from the
, Jolly is a Paladin with a "Very Slow" mana speed that initially scares players away. However, much like his green counterpart
, Jolly is a specialized tool designed to dominate specific game modes. Released in late 2023, he remains a staple in the 3-star meta, particularly when mana speed is set to "Very Fast."

Jolly is a delightful departure from the usual "angry warrior" aesthetic. He’s a stout, bearded gnome lumberjack carrying a bundle of birch wood, sporting a yellow cone hat and antlers. The art screams "festive hardworking gnome," and while he looks harmless, that confident smirk suggests he knows exactly how annoying he is about to make your raid.

Family Bonus, Passives, Aether Power & Unique Abilities
The Winter family is all about minions, which synergizes perfectly with Jolly's kit.
With 2 or 3 heroes (easy to do with
), the minions get beefier. While not game-breaking on its own, it makes the Elf Minions slightly more annoying to clear.
Jolly does not have specific passives, which is typical for his release era.
fits him well. Since his main goal is to survive until he casts, passive healing helps him recover from chip damage without needing a healer to fire first.
Upon release, Jolly was immediately identified as the "Yellow Faiez." He filled a massive gap in the Holy roster for Rush formats.
While his stats have been crept, his
has not. Percentage-based damage reduction (-45%) and mana generation (+35%) are timeless. A 45% reduction is 45% whether the enemy has 500 Attack or 1500 Attack.
He is completely useless in standard raiding due to Very Slow speed. However, in
, he is S-Tier. He is a "must-level" hero solely for these modes.
Jolly is a support tank. He does zero direct damage. There is absolutely no reason to boost his Attack stat.
Maximizes his survivability. The Paladin class talent (Defense buff on damage) synergizes with high defense/health.
path is the only correct choice. Keep him alive so he can keep everyone else alive.
In Very Fast modes, he is one of the best tanks in the game.

He does no damage himself, relying on teammates to kill the enemy.
His buffs (Damage Reduction, Mana Gen) are dispellable. A well-timed
Can be used effectively even against stronger teams due to the power of his buffs.
If you can keep him alive with potions until he fires, his overheal and protection can help complete difficult stages with underpowered teams.
In Rush, you want to fire Jolly early to set up your defenses, then let your damage dealers sweep.
Faiez and Jolly provide dual overheal and ailment immunity/damage reduction. Treevil cripples the enemy. Buster increases damage taken. This team is a timeout nightmare.
Maximize the Winter Family bonus for beefier minions. Frosty adds more minions and healing.

Avoid pairing with other "Very Slow" heroes unless in Rush. In standard play, he will likely die before firing.

If you pull Jolly, lock him immediately. Do not feed him away. While you might not use him in your main map farming team due to his speed, he will be your MVP for Rush Tournaments, which are a great source of loot. Level him up, but maybe prioritize a faster healer (like

You know the drill. Jolly is the yellow Faiez. If you play competitive Rush Wars or want top 1% in tournaments, you need him. Limit breaking him (LB1) is recommended for the stat boost to keep him current. LB2 is a luxury but useful if you use him as a tank in 4-star Rush tournaments to punish purple tanks.

Jolly is a specialist, not a generalist. In the wild world of standard raids, he's a sitting duck. But when the rules change to "Very Fast," he puts on his wizard hat (or gnome hat) and becomes a god. The combination of massive overheal, significant damage reduction, and mana acceleration is simply too much value for a 3-star hero to ignore.
A staple for any serious tournament player's roster.
